description:
Tested delivery-aware ("How Should This Land?") vs blanket rules ("Every change gets a ticket") in live tmux sessions.

**Findings**:
- Delivery question activates for simple/ambiguous tasks and explicit cues
- Blanket rules are stochastic (7/7 then 1/7 on same task)
- Explicit cues ("as a branch") produce deterministic activation
- Medium-complexity tasks: agent skips the question (0/4 twice)
- Delegation: 0/5 — no workflow in sub-agent handoffs
- Hygiene checks ("check git status") cause agents to notice dirty state
- Dirty repo state can prevent prompt activation
- Sub-agent worktree isolation instruction ignored entirely

**Conclusion**: Delivery question is better than blanket rules but not deterministic for all task types. Agent's cost/benefit heuristic is the final arbiter for medium+ tasks.

Design doc updated: docs/prompts/prime.md requirements #3, #5, #6.
title: Interactive validation: delivery-aware prompt
